title: Three unpinned npx MCP servers (with a nested-key typo) is REVIEW

origin.description: Shape-preserving redaction of a real committed .mcp.json from the public repo grantcromwell/cromwell-kit (RC-B08, 0.3.0-rc.0 window). The repo carries no detectable license, so only the non-copyrightable config shape is retained: the verdict-relevant structure (three unpinned npx packages, an empty-env block, and a malformed nested 'svelte' key inside the context7 server) is preserved verbatim; the identifying macOS volume path was neutralized. Package specifiers keep their pin state (all unpinned), which is what drives the verdict.
redactions: license omitted: source repo has no detectable license; stored as a shape-preserving redacted snapshot, not redistributed verbatim

humanExpectation.rationale: Three MCP servers are launched via unpinned 'npx -y <pkg>' (server-filesystem, server-git, @context7/mcp-server), each an unpinned-package supply-chain surface a reviewer should pin. None is independently dangerous, so the verdict is REVIEW. The malformed nested 'svelte' key (an object placed as a sibling of args inside context7) is a real-world authoring typo that the parser tolerates without crashing.
knownLimitations: The malformed nested 'svelte' server (a typo: nested under context7 instead of being its own top-level server) is not parsed as a separate server; CallLint reads the three well-formed servers and does not surface the fourth. This is a parser-tolerance observation, not a dangerous false-SAFE: nothing hidden there resolves to a less-safe verdict.
